title: Evangelion and felix culpa and other theological concepts
display_title: Evangelion and felix culpa and other theological concepts
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion and felix culpa (and other theological concepts)             Quote Post by Atropos » Fri Jun 20, 2014 6:47 pm The official statement by the creators of NGE is that the Judeo-Christian symbolism is window-dressing. In accordance with this statement, most fan analysis thus far has been focused on other symbols, mostly psychological or archetypal. HOWEVER! Some aspects of NGE do concur with areas of Christian philosophy. "Felix culpa" (lucky fault) is one such idea. Felix culpa, as codified by St. Augustine, supposes that the Fall of Man was intentional on the part of God and for mankind's betterment : it allowed man to understand the difference between good and evil. Thus, the Original Sin actually brought mankind closer to God. In NGE, Gendo and SEELE are both trying, in their own ways, to return to Eden. By destroying the barriers that differentiate individuals, they hope to reach paradise on Earth. However, as the characters discover during Instrumentality, the removal of pain also means the removal of all aspects of individual thought—leaving individuals within Instrumentality as mindless as Adam and Eve were before eating the Fruit of Knowledge. In the end of EoE, Shinji chooses to accept the burden of the AT Field—the "Knowledge of Good and Evil", which are, essentially, different ways of interacting with other people—and, in doing so, is cast out of paradise. His subsequent juxtaposition with Asuka on the beach marks him as the second Adam (no, not that one), leading mankind into lucidity and, eventually, to wisdom. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Fri Jun 20, 2014 7:16 pm Honestly I see some cool religious ideas in there, but it's non canon stuff like; Lilith, Angels being scary tall, Sammael, Rapture, Spear of Longinus, Chamber of Guf, etc etc. Adam in NGE has more in common with the idea of The Light Bringer than he does with an actual man. NTE brings up some awesome Revelation type imagery.

[]: Re: Evangelion and felix culpa (and other theological concep             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Wed Jul 02, 2014 12:58 pm Atropos wrote: In the end of EoE, Shinji chooses to accept the burden of the AT Field—the "Knowledge of Good and Evil", which are, essentially, different ways of interacting with other people—and, in doing so, is cast out of paradise.

[]: Re: Evangelion and felix culpa (and other theological concep             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Wed Jul 02, 2014 5:18 pm Atropos wrote: Well, there's my lackluster example! Anyone else got comparisons between NGE and actual beliefs in Abrahamic religions?

[]: Re: Evangelion and felix culpa (and other theological concep             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 02, 2014 5:42 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: I can't think of a show like EVA that deals with that idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sat Dec 13, 2014 11:22 am Animerica Magazine wrote: Anno says the new offering from Gainax will consider some of the ultimate questions posed by science fiction, and, indeed, philosophy, such as: What is the nature of evolution? What is humanity's relationship to his or her God? Does god, in fact, exist? What does it mean for the human race if that question can be answered definitively?
